title: BNCI 2015-012 PASS2D P300 dataset
description: This dataset comprises a 9-class auditory event-related potential (ERP) paradigm designed for brain-computer interface (BCI) applications, specifically a predictive text entry system called PASS2D. Ten healthy participants performed a single session including calibration and online spelling tasks using two-dimensional auditory stimuli varying in pitch and spatial direction. The dataset includes 63-channel EEG recordings preprocessed with bandpass filtering, downsampling to 100 Hz, and artifact rejection, achieving an average spelling speed of 0.8 characters per minute.
methods_description: Data were acquired at 250 Hz from 63 EEG channels using wet Ag/AgCl electrodes in a 10-20 montage (Brain Products hardware). Auditory stimuli (708, 524, 380 Hz tones) were presented via headphones with 125 ms inter-stimulus interval. Preprocessing included 0.1-250 Hz analog bandpass filtering followed by 40 Hz digital lowpass filtering, downsampling to 100 Hz, and artifact rejection (threshold: 100 μV peak-to-peak). Classification used Fisher discriminant analysis with shrinkage regularization on mean amplitudes from N200 (230-300 ms) and P300 (350+ ms) intervals.
